texto: What could I do to feel happier living here? … 1. Walk more. 2. Buy local.3. Get to know my neighbors.4. Do fun stuff.5. Explore nature.6. Volunteer.7. Eat local.8. Become more political.9. Create something new.10. Stay loyal through hard times.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Melody Warnick que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “What could I do to feel happier living here? … 1. Walk more. 2. Buy local.3. Get to know my neigh…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, home, local, com foco em reflexao.

frase_original: What could I do to feel happier living here? … 1. Walk more. 2. Buy local.3. Get to know my neighbors.4. Do fun stuff.5. Explore nature.6. Volunteer.7. Eat local.8. Become more political.9. Create something new.10. Stay loyal through hard times.

texto: We speak of searching for happiness, of finding contentment, as if these were locations on an atlas, actual places that we could visit if only we had the proper map and the right navigational skills.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Melody Warnick: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a felicidade. O trecho “We speak of searching for happiness, of finding contentment, as if these were locations on an atl…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que felicidade, contentment, happiness pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de happiness e place.
